summary: Large decomp-matching PR for `src/melee/mp/mpcoll.c`, described by the author as "frustratingly close!". The decomp.dev report credited +22136 matched bytes, +0.57% matched code, 24 newly matching `main/melee/mp/mpcoll` functions, and 2 further improvements, with `mpColl_80046224_LeftWall` still only 99.81%. The patch filled in most of `mpcoll.c`, renamed many collision routines by surface role, tightened `CollData` field names, renamed `mpColl_8004CC00` to `mpCopyCollData`, and corrected `mpLib_80051EC8`'s prototype/call sites to take segment coordinates before output pointers.
what_changed: Replaced a large block of previously unmatched or weakly matched `mpcoll.c` code with matching C, adding 2606 lines and deleting 615 lines in that file.
Renamed address-only `mpColl` functions in `symbols.txt` and headers with direction/surface suffixes such as `_Floor`, `_Ceiling`, `_RightWall`, and `_LeftWall`.

Renamed `mpColl_8004CC00` to `mpCopyCollData` and implemented it as explicit `CollData` field copying, including ECBs, positions, flags, ledge ids, wall/floor/ceiling collision records, and `lstick_x`.

Renamed `CollData` fields in `src/melee/lb/types.h`: `ledge_id_unk0` to `ledge_id_right`, `ledge_id_unk1` to `ledge_id_left`, and `x60` to `lstick_x`; call sites in fighter/cliff code were updated accordingly.

Changed `mpLib_80051EC8` from output-pointer-first ordering to coordinate-first ordering: `float x0, float y0, float x1, float y1, Vec3* pos_out, int* line_id_out, u32* flags_out, Vec3* normal_out, u32 arg4, int joint_id0, int joint_id1`.
Updated `mpLib_80051EC8` call sites in stage, Flat Zone, item, and Master Hand laser code to the corrected argument order.
Added `MPCOLL_WALLID_MAX 9` in `src/melee/mp/forward.h` and used it with wall-id assertions in `mpcoll.c`.
Introduced `struct mpColl_80458810_t` with `right[9]`, `left[9]`, and a `Vec3 normal`, matching collision-side data around `mpColl_80458810`.
Renamed sbss symbols `mpColl_804D6490`, `mpColl_804D6494`, and `mpColl_804D6498` to `mpColl_804D6490_max_x`, `mpColl_804D6494_line_id`, and `mpColl_804D6498_flags`.
smart_moves: Used semantic suffixes on address-based function names to document the actual surface being tested while preserving original addresses, making collision code searchable without pretending full names are known.
Corrected `mpLib_80051EC8`'s parameter order globally rather than locally hacking call sites; the coordinate-first signature matches how the body uses the start/end segment values.

Promoted ambiguous `CollData` fields only when call-site evidence supported the meaning: ledge ids were named left/right based on cliff and air-catch usage, and `x60` became `lstick_x` where fighter input assigns `fp->input.lstick.x`.

Kept exact types and bool return types in `mpcoll.h` close to observed use, replacing many `s32`/`UNK_RET` prototypes with `bool` and `int* line_id_out` where the implementation and callers supported it.
Centralized wall-id array sizing with `MPCOLL_WALLID_MAX` instead of leaving a literal `9` embedded only in assertion/string data.
Implemented `mpCopyCollData` by explicit field assignment, which exposes the real copy semantics and avoids assuming it is a raw struct copy.
decomp_lessons: For collision systems, adding `_Floor`, `_Ceiling`, `_LeftWall`, and `_RightWall` suffixes to still-address-based functions can unlock readability and matching without requiring final retail names.
When a function signature looks unnatural and causes awkward call sites, inspect the function body and all callers; a prototype reorder can be the matching breakthrough.
Field names should be propagated through all dependent systems after being inferred; this PR updated fighter, ledge, stage, and item call sites rather than leaving mixed `unk` and named fields.
Bot match reports are useful post-merge evidence: this PR's report listed 24 new matches and one almost-matched function, confirming the work was broad matching progress rather than pure cleanup.
Do not overgeneralize explicit copy helpers as memcpy; `mpCopyCollData` copies a curated subset/order of fields and has a special `arg2 == 1` branch.
Naming sbss scratch globals by observed role, such as `max_x`, `line_id`, and `flags`, improves future line-intersection decomp work.
naming_conventions: `mpColl_<address>_RightWall` and `_LeftWall` for directional wall routines, e.g. `mpColl_80044E10_RightWall`, `mpColl_800454A4_RightWall`, `mpColl_80048AB0_RightWall`, `mpColl_80049778_LeftWall`, `mpColl_80049EAC_LeftWall`, `mpColl_8004BDD4_LeftWall`.
Use `_out` suffix for pointer output parameters such as `line_id_out`, `pos_out`, `flags_out`, and `normal_out`.
Name ledge ids by side in `CollData`: `ledge_id_right` and `ledge_id_left`.
Name input-derived collision fields directly, e.g. `lstick_x` for the field assigned from `fp->input.lstick.x`.
Use descriptive non-address names when behavior is clear, e.g. `mpCopyCollData` for `mpColl_8004CC00`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Large matching gain came from replacing address-only stubs/unmatched blocks with real C for many `mpcoll.c` functions, especially surface-specific collision handlers.
Float literal style was normalized to `F` suffixes in touched code, likely to preserve expected single-precision codegen in matching contexts.
The patch retained some `// TODO: inhibit inlining` comments around collision logic, indicating inlining control remained relevant for exact codegen.
Global prototype correction for `mpLib_80051EC8` reduced argument shuffling at every call site and aligned the C source with the internal distance calculations from the segment start point.
Explicit field copying in `mpCopyCollData` can be reused as a matching tactic when a helper copies a struct subset in a nontrivial order.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported +22136 matched bytes, 24 new matching functions in `main/melee/mp/mpcoll`, and 2 improvements in unmatched functions.
The bot specifically reported `mpColl_80046224_LeftWall` improved from 0.00% to 99.81%, supporting the PR title's "almost fully" wording.
follow_up_queries: Find the later PR that finishes `mpColl_80046224_LeftWall` from 99.81% to 100%.
Search for later renames of address-based `mpColl_*_Floor`, `_Ceiling`, `_LeftWall`, and `_RightWall` functions to more semantic names.
Check whether `mpLib_80051EC8` received a final descriptive name after this coordinate-first signature correction.
Look for follow-up cleanup of `mpColl_80458810_t` and the wall-id arrays using `MPCOLL_WALLID_MAX`.
Search for remaining `TODO: inhibit inlining` comments in `mpcoll.c` after PR #1972.
